LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO BRING TO TRIAL IS GRANTED. DISMISSAL IS MANDATORY PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 583.360 SINCE THIS CASE HAS BEEN PENDING MORE THAN FIVE YEARS AND THERE IS NO BASIS TO EXTEND THE FIVE YEAR PERIOD OR TO FIND THAT ANY TIME HAS BEEN TOLLED. PETITIONER DAVID PILPEL'S ARGUMENT THAT THE SECTION 583.360 DOES NOT APPLY TO CEQA CASES IS CONTRARY TO THE DEFINITION OF THE WORD "ACTION" IN SECTION 583.110 AND IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MS. PEARSON WILL PREPARE A JUDGMENT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Petitioner David Pilpel telephone number 415-977-5578 in Propria Persona.
Audrey Pearson of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4621 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial is granted. Dismissal is mandatory per Code of Civil Procedure 583.360 since this case has been pending more than five years and there is no basis to extend the five year period or to find that any time has been tolled. Petitioner David Pilpel's argument that the section 583.360 does not apply to CEQA cases is contrary to the definition of the word "action" in section 583.110 and is not supported by any authority. Order signed in open Court. Ms. Pearson will prepare a judgment.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Respondent City and County of San Francisco's motion to dismiss for failure to bring to trial will be heard in Department 302, Hon. Harold E. Kahn presiding. Department 302 may issue its own tentative ruling. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)
